page: Microsoft Azure E32s_v6 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine belonging to the Esv6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated virtual CPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 16 physical cores and 2 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The processor is equipped with a 260 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. It includes advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and INT8 operations.

The instance is configured with 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage, but it is Premium Storage capable, allowing users to attach external storage volumes. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are bundled with this instance type, and it does not feature any GPU hardware.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong capabilities in specific workloads. The E32s_v6 demonstrates strong single-threaded performance in gzip compression (30.93 GB/sec) and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression (180.09 GB/sec). Geekbench results show strong single-core performance (2001.0) alongside strong image and text processing capabilities. In LLM inference tests using a small 135M model, it achieves a top-tier text generation speed of 390.88 tokens/sec, though its Passmark memory score is categorized as weak.

Given its high memory density of 8 GB per vCPU and dedicated Intel Xeon compute resources, the E32s_v6 offers a balanced cost-to-resource profile for memory-intensive enterprise workloads without the overhead of local storage. It is well-suited for memory-optimized applications, database operations, static web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference tasks that leverage AMX instructions.
description: Microsoft Azure E32s_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Esv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 16 physical cores operating at 4.2 GHz, backed by a 260 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM, providing a high 8.0 GB per vCPU ratio, though it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark data highlights strong single-threaded gzip compression at 30.93 GB/sec, strong multi-threaded bzip3 decompression at 180.09 GB/sec, and top-tier text generation speeds of 390.88 tokens/sec for small LLM models. While its Passmark memory score is relatively weak, its dedicated compute and support for Intel AMX and AVX-512 instructions make it highly suitable for memory-intensive enterprise workloads, database operations, static web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E32s_v6 is a memory-optimized VM featuring 32 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and advanced AMX instructions, delivering strong performance for databases and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E32s_v6 is a memory-optimized VM with 32 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and support for AVX-512 and AMX instructions.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and advanced matrix extensions for high-throughput enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor at 4.2 GHz
256.0 GB of system memory providing a high 8.0 GB per vCPU ratio
Supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated computing
Strong performance in single-threaded compression and small LLM text generation
Premium Storage capable configuration with no local storage or GPUs
Optimized for memory-intensive databases, web serving, and enterprise applications
